page: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EADSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 8 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It also includes 2458 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in single-core CPU tasks, as shown by stress-ng single-core results in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for cached small blocks and uncached large block reads, but large block write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Large language model inference speeds are average for prompt processing but strong for text generation using a small 135M model.

This server offers high memory density and substantial local storage capacity, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. The primary tradeoff is the lower multi-core performance and weak large-block write memory bandwidth relative to its massive memory footprint. It is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EADSv5 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, alongside 512.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage. The server features a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core and is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark evaluations show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small LLM models, while multi-core CPU, database operations, and memory read bandwidth perform in the average range. However, large-block write memory bandwidth is weak. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, making it suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and data-intensive enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ads_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high memory density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and substantial local storage for data-intensive workloads.

Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 32.0 GB per core ratio
Includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM text generation

Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
